name: Hani ibn Hani
title: Messenger of Letters
shortDescription: One of Kufa's ambassadors who delivered the second batch of invitation letters from the people of Kufa to Imam Hussain (AS) in Mecca.
fullStory:
Hani ibn Hani al-Sabi'i was a brave and trusted Shia from Kufa. When the people of Kufa decided to invite Imam Hussain (AS) to lead their city, they wrote numerous letters.

Hani ibn Hani, along with Sa'id ibn Abdullah al-Hanafi, was tasked with delivering the second batch of letters from the Kufans, which included over 150 letters from the city's elders, to the Imam. They traveled quickly and secretly from Kufa towards Mecca.

They reached the Imam's presence in Mecca and presented the letters. It was after reading these letters that the Imam decided to send Muslim ibn Aqeel as his special ambassador and representative to Kufa.

By fulfilling this critical mission, Hani ibn Hani recorded his name in history as one of the main and trusted messengers of the Ashura movement.
quote: (Part of the Kufans' message he brought:) We have sent our best to you, so come to us...
